title: This is the new 18-in-1 electric vehicle you can build yourself
summary:
If you’ve ever wished you could buy one electric vehicle that could evolve with your needs instead of replacing it every time your use case changes, a Canadian company thinks it has the answer.

ENVO Drive Systems has unveiled its new Utility Personal Transporter (UPT), a modular electric vehicle platform that can be configured into as many as 18 different vehicle types, with even more combinations possible through optional accessories and upgrades.

title: NEVI gets real in Illinois as first federally funded EV chargers go online
summary: The Illinois Department of Transportation (IDOT) cut the ribbon on the state’s first federally-funded EV charging station this week, after energizing new high-speed chargers at a Phillips 66 Mach 1 station in Casey, IL.

title: The new Hyundai Kona leaks with a tougher look and a surprise inside
summary: Hyundai skipped the typical mid-cycle refresh and instead gave the Kona a complete overhaul. The first image of it leaked, revealing a taller, SUV-like design.

title: Schneider Electric’s upgraded app-free EV charger now has NACS
summary: Schneider Electric has just given its Charge Pro commercial Level 2 EV charger a sweet upgrade that includes NACS support, app-free charging, and a quicker setup process.

title: BYD launches the Denza Z9 GT EV in Europe and the UK at over 3X the price
summary: BYD launched its luxury sub-brand Denza in Europe with a little help from James Bond himself. The Denza Z9 GT EV can recharge in as little as 5 minutes, drive nearly 400 miles on a single charge, and looks like something straight out of an action movie.

title: Massachusetts wants to pay EV drivers for their battery power
summary: Some Massachusetts EV owners will soon be able to earn incentives for sending electricity from their car batteries back to the grid when demand spikes.

title: Heybike Mars 2.0, ALPHA, Horizon e-bikes get up to $800 exclusive savings to new lows, Segway EVs up to 46% off, Aventon, more
summary:
We’ve got an EV-focused edition of Green Deals for you today, starting with up to $800 exclusive savings you can score on Heybike’s Mars 2.0 Folding e-bike, ALPHA All-Terrain Mid-Drive e-bike, and Horizon Full-Suspension e-bike – all at new low prices starting from $799, after using our code at checkout. We also have the newly launched Segway Back to School EV Sale with up to 46% savings across e-scooters, e-bikes, and the GoKart Pro 2, with this sale offering the second-ever discount on the GT3 Pro SuperScooter to its $2,500 low. There’s also the all-time low prices on Aventon’s Level 3 Commuter and Pace 4 Cruiser e-bikes, and the EGO Power+ 2,100 PSI Cordless Electric Pressure Washer bundle with a 6.0Ah battery, as well as the Schumacher NACS to J1772 EV charger adapter, a VIOFO two-channel dash cam system, and more. As always, you can find all the hangover deals from this week down at the bottom of the page, like yesterday’s Anker SOLIX Summer Camp Outlet Sale with exclusive bonus savings, and more.

Head below for other New Green Deals we’ve found today and, of course, Electrek’s best EV buying and leasing deals. Also, check out the new Electrek Tesla Shop for the best deals on Tesla accessories.

title: Toyota warns it ‘cannot survive’ as the next-gen Corolla EV becomes its make-or-break
summary: The world’s best-selling car of all time is going electric. Toyota warned it “cannot survive” by sticking to its traditional methods as it pins its future on the next-generation Corolla EV.

title: Volvo’s new midsize electric SUV delivers more range than expected, with up to 330 miles
summary: Volvo is already calling it a game-changing electric SUV. The new 2027 EX60 now has an EPA-estimated driving range of up to 330 miles, making it the longest-range EV Volvo offers.

title: Electrek Formula Sun Grand Prix 2026: Day 2 results and images
summary:
The second day of racing at this year’s Electrek Formula Sun Grand Prix concluded late last night, with the finalization of lap results. The third and final day of racing is now underway, with 30 teams cleared for the track and 12 qualified to compete in the cross-country Electrek American Solar Challenge.

I expect the latter number to increase significantly by the end of the day.

title: A Bill Taking Aim At Chinese Cars In America Just Advanced In The Senate. It Could Hit Mercedes Too
summary: The Senate has moved forward legislation that would ban automakers with too much Chinese ownership.

title: Ford Is Switching To Apple Maps For Its Next-Gen EV Navigation
summary: Apple Maps in Ford's upcoming $30,000 electric truck will help with route planning and provide directions to Blue Cruise.

title: Subaru's Biggest EV Yet Just Got Delayed
summary: The American-made Getaway three-row SUV was supposed to go into production by the end of the year.

title: Tesla Sold 25% More Cars In Q2. Its Operating Profit Still Plummeted
summary: Record revenue and a big delivery rebound weren’t enough to offset lower vehicle margins and Tesla’s costly AI and robotics push.

title: Ford Just Made It Easier To Back Up Your House With An F-150 Hybrid Or Lightning
summary: The new solution means you don't need to use a mess of extension cords to back up your fridge or other appliances.

title: Tesla Is Recalling 15 Cars For Being Too Canadian
summary: Tesla accidentally sold 15 customers cars that it imported from Canada, which means they don't meet federal safety standards.

title: The Tesla Diner Was The World's Most Utilized Supercharger In Its First Year
summary: The Tesla Diner delivered more electricity than any other Supercharger station in the world, averaging 1,600 sessions per day.

title: BYD Wants To Take the World's Fastest Car Record... From Itself
summary: BYD's own Yangwang U9 Xtreme already holds the production car top speed record, but it's determined to beat that soon.

title: EVs Just Hit 25% Market Share In Europe
summary: Electric cars accounted for 25.6% of June registrations across 17 European markets, as sales jumped nearly 40%.

title: Tesla Has To Fix Its Super-Bright Headlights. A New Law Could Change Things For Good.
summary: Tesla called its uber-bright headlights "inconsequential" to safety. Now the feds are making it fix the problem.
